title: Blueprint and Schematic Interpreter
summary: Learners move between plan view, one-line view, symbol legend, and room-level 3D overlays to connect drawings with real installation locations and circuit intent.
learningObjectives: Read common electrical symbols, legends, and panel references.
Trace branch circuits from plan view into physical room locations.
Use schedules and notes to plan installation or troubleshooting work.

environment.setting: Digital drafting table with house plan, panel schedule, symbol legend, and 3D room overlay
environment.focus: Turning drawings into installable field work

interactables.label: Electrical symbol legend
interactables.teaches: Defines receptacle, switch, luminaire, homerun, and panel symbols.

interactables.label: Room-by-room circuit overlay
interactables.teaches: Links drawing symbols to physical device locations in the 3D model.

interactables.label: Panel and circuit schedule
interactables.teaches: Connects drawing callouts to breaker numbers and load assignments.

interactables.label: Circuit trace mode
interactables.teaches: Lets the learner follow a branch circuit from the panel to the final device.

tasks: Match symbols on the plan to their correct field devices.
Trace a selected homerun back to the correct panel and breaker.
Identify which room devices share the same branch circuit.
Use the schedule and notes to prepare a simple installation sequence.
completion: Learner reads symbols and schedules accurately.
Learner traces circuits from plan to panel without errors.
Learner explains how the drawings guide installation work.
